49.2: High and low, rich and poor, * one with another.
49.3: My mouth shall speak of wisdom, * and my heart shall muse of understanding.
49.4: I will incline mine ear to the parable, * and show my dark speech upon the harp.
49.5: Wherefore should I fear in the days of evil, * when wickedness at my heels compasseth me round about?
49.6: There be some that put their trust in their goods, * and boast themselves in the multitude of their riches.
49.7: But no man may deliver his brother, * nor give a ransom unto God for him,
49.8: (For it cost more to redeem their souls, * so that he must let that alone for ever;)
49.9: That he shall live alway, * and not see the grave.
49.10: For he seeth that wise men also die and perish together, * as well as the ignorant and foolish, and leave their riches for other.
49.11: And yet they think that their houses shall continue for ever, and that their dwelling-places shall endure from one generation to another; * and call the lands after their own names.
49.12: Nevertheless, man being in honour abideth not, * seeing he may be compared unto the beasts that perish;
49.13: This their way is very foolishness; * yet their posterity praise their saying.
49.14: They lie in the grave like sheep; death is their shepherd; and the righteous shall have dominion over them in the morning: * their beauty shall consume in the sepulchre, and have no abiding.
49.15: But God hath delivered my soul from the power of the grave; * for he shall receive me.
49.16: Be not thou afraid, though one be made rich, * or if the glory of his house be increased;
49.17: For he shall carry nothing away with him when he dieth, * neither shall his pomp follow him.
49.18: For while he lived, he counted himself an happy man; * and so long as thou doest well unto thyself, men will speak good of thee.
49.19: He shall follow the generation of his fathers, * and shall never see light.
49.20: Man that is in honour but hath no understanding * is compared unto the beasts that perish.

50.1: The LORD, even the Most Mighty God, hath spoken, * and called the world, from the rising up of the sun unto the going down thereof.
50.2: Out of Sion hath God appeared * in perfect beauty.
50.3: Our God shall come, and shall not keep silence; * there shall go before him a consuming fire, and a mighty tempest shall be stirred up round about him.
50.4: He shall call the heaven from above, * and the earth, that he may judge his people.
50.5: Gather my saints together unto me; * those that have made a covenant with me with sacrifice.
50.6: And the heavens shall declare his righteousness; * for God is Judge himself.
50.7: Hear, O my people, and I will speak; * I myself will testify against thee, O Israel; for I am God, even thy God.
50.8: I will not reprove thee because of thy sacrifices; * as for thy burnt-offerings, they are alway before me.
50.9: I will take no bullock out of thine house, * nor he-goats out of thy folds.
50.10: For all the beasts of the forest are mine, * and so are the cattle upon a thousand hills.
50.11: I know all the fowls upon the mountains, * and the wild beasts of the field are in my sight.
50.12: If I be hungry, I will not tell thee; * for the whole world is mine, and all that is therein.
50.13: Thinkest thou that I will eat bulls' flesh, * and drink the blood of goats?
50.14: Offer unto God thanksgiving, * and pay thy vows unto the Most Highest.
50.15: And call upon me in the time of trouble; * so will I hear thee, and thou shalt praise me.
50.16: But unto the ungodly saith God, * Why dost thou preach my laws, and takest my covenant in thy mouth;
50.17: Whereas thou hatest to be reformed, * and hast cast my words behind thee?
50.18: When thou sawest a thief, thou consentedst unto him; * and hast been partaker with the adulterers.
50.19: Thou hast let thy mouth speak wickedness, * and with thy tongue thou hast set forth deceit.
50.20: Thou sattest and spakest against thy brother; * yea, and hast slandered thine own mother's son.
50.21: These things hast thou done, and I held my tongue, and thou thoughtest wickedly, that I am even such a one as thyself; * but I will reprove thee, and set before thee the things that thou hast done.
50.22: O consider this, ye that forget God, * lest I pluck you away, and there be none to deliver you.
50.23: Whoso offereth me thanks and praise, he honoureth me; * and to him that ordereth his way aright, will I show the salvation of God.

51.1: Have mercy upon me, O God, after thy great goodness; * according to the multitude of thy mercies do away mine offences.
51.2: Wash me throughly from my wickedness, * and cleanse me from my sin.
51.3: For I acknowledge my faults, * and my sin is ever before me.
51.4: Against thee only have I sinned, and done this evil in thy sight; * that thou mightest be justified in thy saying, and clear when thou shalt judge.
51.5: Behold, I was shapen in wickedness, * and in sin hath my mother conceived me.
51.6: But lo, thou requirest truth in the inward parts, * and shalt make me to understand wisdom secretly.
51.7: Thou shalt purge me with hyssop, and I shall be clean; * thou shalt wash me, and I shall be whiter than snow.
51.8: Thou shalt make me hear of joy and gladness, * that the bones which thou hast broken may rejoice.
51.9: Turn thy face from my sins, * and put out all my misdeeds.
51.10: Make me a clean heart, O God, * and renew a right spirit within me.
51.11: Cast me not away from thy presence, * and take not thy holy Spirit from me.
51.12: O give me the comfort of thy help again, * and stablish me with thy free Spirit.
51.13: Then shall I teach thy ways unto the wicked, * and sinners shall be converted unto thee.
51.14: Deliver me from blood-guiltiness, O God, thou that art the God of my health; * and my tongue shall sing of thy righteousness.
51.15: Thou shalt open my lips, O Lord, * and my mouth shall show thy praise.
51.16: For thou desirest no sacrifice, else would I give it thee; * but thou delightest not in burnt-offerings.
51.17: The sacrifice of God is a troubled spirit: * a broken and contrite heart, O God, shalt thou not despise.
51.18: O be favourable and gracious unto Sion; * build thou the walls of Jerusalem.
51.19: Then shalt thou be pleased with the sacrifice of righteousness, with the burnt-offerings and oblations; * then shall they offer young bullocks upon thine altar.

52.1: Why boastest thou thyself, thou tyrant, * that thou canst do mischief;
52.2: Whereas the goodness of God * endureth yet daily?
52.3: Thy tongue imagineth wickedness, * and with lies thou cuttest like a sharp razor.
52.4: Thou hast loved unrighteousness more than goodness, * and falsehood more than righteousness.
52.5: Thou hast loved to speak all words that may do hurt, * O thou false tongue.
52.6: Therefore shall God destroy thee for ever; * he shall take thee, and pluck thee out of thy dwelling, and root thee out of the land of the living.
52.7: The righteous also shall see this, and fear, * and shall laugh him to scorn:
52.8: Lo, this is the man that took not God for his strength; * but trusted unto the multitude of his riches, and strengthened himself in his wickedness.
52.9: As for me, I am like a green olive-tree in the house of God; * my trust is in the tender mercy of God for ever and ever.
52.10: I will alway give thanks unto thee for that thou hast done; * and I will hope in thy Name, for thy saints like it well.

53.1: The foolish body hath said in his heart, * There is no God.
53.2: Corrupt are they, and become abominable in their wickedness; * there is none that doeth good.
53.3: God looked down from heaven upon the children of men, * to see if there were any that would understand, and seek after God.
53.4: But they are all gone out of the way, they are altogether become abominable; * there is also none that doeth good, no not one.
53.5: Are not they without understanding that work wickedness, * eating up my people as if they would eat bread? they have not called upon God.
53.6: They were afraid where no fear was; * for God hath broken the bones of him that besieged thee; thou hast put them to confusion, because God hath despised them.
53.7: O that the salvation were given unto Israel out of Sion! * O that the Lord would deliver his people out of captivity!
53.8: Then should Jacob rejoice, * and Israel should be right glad.

54.1: Save me, O God, for thy Name's sake, * and avenge me in thy strength.
54.2: Hear my prayer, O God, * and hearken unto the words of my mouth.
54.3: For strangers are risen up against me; * and tyrants, which have not God before their eyes, seek after my soul.
54.4: Behold, God is my helper; * the Lord is with them that uphold my soul.
54.5: He shall reward evil unto mine enemies: * destroy thou them in thy truth.
54.6: An offering of a free heart will I give thee, and praise thy Name, O LORD; * because it is so comfortable.
54.7: For he hath delivered me out of all my trouble; * and mine eye hath seen his desire upon mine enemies.

55.1: Hear my prayer, O God, * and hide not thyself from my petition.
55.2: Take heed unto me, and hear me, * how I mourn in my prayer, and am vexed;
55.3: The enemy crieth so, and the ungodly cometh on so fast; * for they are minded to do me some mischief, so maliciously are they set against me.
55.4: My heart is disquieted within me, * and the fear of death is fallen upon me.
55.5: Fearfulness and trembling are come upon me, * and an horrible dread hath overwhelmed me.
55.6: And I said, O that I had wings like a dove! * for then would I flee away, and be at rest.
55.7: Lo, then would I get me away far off, * and remain in the wilderness.
55.8: I would make haste to escape, * because of the stormy wind and tempest.
55.9: Destroy their tongues, O Lord, and divide them; * for I have spied unrighteousness and strife in the city.
55.10: Day and night they go about within the walls thereof: * mischief also and sorrow are in the midst of it.
55.11: Wickedness is therein; * deceit and guile go not out of her streets.
55.12: For it is not an open enemy that hath done me this dishonour; * for then I could have borne it;
55.13: Neither was it mine adversary that did magnify himself against me; * for then peradventure I would have hid myself from him;
55.14: But it was even thou, my companion, * my guide, and mine own familiar friend.
55.15: We took sweet counsel together, * and walked in the house of God as friends.
55.16: Let death come hastily upon them, and let them go down alive into the pit; * for wickedness is in their dwellings, and among them.
55.17: As for me, I will call upon God, * and the LORD shall save me.
55.18: In the evening, and morning, and at noon-day will I pray, and that instantly; * and he shall hear my voice.
55.19: It is he that hath delivered my soul in peace from the battle that was against me; * for there were many that strove with me.
55.20: Yea, even God, that endureth for ever, shall hear me, and bring them down; * for they will not turn, nor fear God.
55.21: He laid his hands upon such as be at peace with him, * and he brake his covenant.
55.22: The words of his mouth were softer than butter, having war in his heart; * his words were smoother than oil, and yet be they very swords.
55.23: O cast thy burden upon the LORD, and he shall nourish thee, * and shall not suffer the righteous to fall for ever.
55.24: And as for them, * thou, O God, shalt bring them into the pit of destruction.
55.25: The blood-thirsty and deceitful men shall not live out half their days: * nevertheless, my trust shall be in thee, O Lord.

56.1: Be merciful unto me, O God, for man goeth about to devour me; * he is daily fighting, and troubling me.
56.2: Mine enemies are daily at hand to swallow me up; * for they be many that fight against me, O thou Most Highest.
56.3: Nevertheless, though I am sometime afraid, * yet put I my trust in thee.
56.4: I will praise God, because of his word: * I have put my trust in God, and will not fear what flesh can do unto me.
56.5: They daily mistake my words; * all that they imagine is to do me evil.
56.6: They hold all together, and keep themselves close, * and mark my steps, when they lay wait for my soul.
56.7: Shall they escape for their wickedness? * thou, O God, in thy displeasure shalt cast them down.
56.8: Thou tellest my wanderings; put my tears into thy bottle: * are not these things noted in thy book?
56.9: Whensoever I call upon thee, then shall mine enemies be put to flight: * this I know; for God is on my side.
56.10: In God's word will I rejoice; * in the LORD ' S word will I comfort me.
56.11: Yea, in God have I put my trust; * I will not be afraid what man can do unto me.
56.12: Unto thee, O God, will I pay my vows; * unto thee will I give thanks.
56.13: For thou hast delivered my soul from death, and my feet from falling, * that I may walk before God in the light of the living.

57.1: Be merciful unto me, O God, be merciful unto me; for my soul trusteth in thee; * and under the shadow of thy wings shall be my refuge, until this tyranny be overpast.
57.2: I will call unto the Most High God, * even unto the God that shall perform the cause which I have in hand.
57.3: He shall send from heaven, * and save me from the reproof of him that would eat me up.
57.4: God shall send forth his mercy and truth: * my soul is among lions;
57.5: And I lie even among the children of men, that are set on fire, * whose teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword.
57.6: Set up thyself, O God, above the heavens; * and thy glory above all the earth.
57.7: They have laid a net for my feet, and pressed down my soul; * they have digged a pit before me, and are fallen into the midst of it themselves.
57.8: My heart is fixed, O God, my heart is fixed; * I will sing and give praise.
57.9: Awake up, my glory; awake, lute and harp: * I myself will awake right early.
57.10: I will give thanks unto thee, O Lord, among the peoples; * and I will sing unto thee among the nations.
57.11: For the greatness of thy mercy reacheth unto the heavens, * and thy truth unto the clouds.
57.12: Set up thyself, O God, above the heavens; * and thy glory above all the earth.

58.1: Are your minds set upon righteousness, O ye congregation? * and do ye judge the thing that is right, O ye sons of men?
58.2: Yea, ye imagine mischief in your heart upon the earth, * and your hands deal with wickedness.
58.3: The ungodly are froward, even from their mother's womb; * as soon as they are born, they go astray, and speak lies.
58.4: They are as venomous as the poison of a serpent, * even like the deaf adder, that stoppeth her ears;
58.5: Which refuseth to hear the voice of the charmer, * charm he never so wisely.
58.6: Break their teeth, O God, in their mouths; * smite the jaw-bones of the lions, O LORD.
58.7: Let them fall away like water that runneth apace; * when they shoot their arrows, let them be rooted out.
58.8: Let them consume away like a snail, and be like the untimely fruit of a woman; * and let them not see the sun.
58.9: Or ever your pots be made hot with thorns, * he shall take them away with a whirlwind, the green and the burning alike.
58.10: The righteous shall rejoice when he seeth the vengeance; * he shall wash his footsteps in the blood of the ungodly.
58.11: So that a man shall say, Verily there is a reward for the righteous; * doubtless there is a God that judgeth the earth.

59.1: Deliver me from mine enemies, O God; * defend me from them that rise up against me.
59.2: O deliver me from the wicked doers, * and save me from the blood-thirsty men.
59.3: For lo, they lie waiting for my soul; * the mighty men are gathered against me, without any offence or fault of me, O LORD.
59.4: They run and prepare themselves without my fault; * arise thou therefore to help me, and behold.
59.5: Stand up, O LORD God of hosts, thou God of Israel, to visit all the heathen, * and be not merciful unto them that offend of malicious wickedness.
59.6: They go to and fro in the evening, * they grin like a dog, and run about through the city.
59.7: Behold, they speak with their mouth, and swords are in their lips; * for who doth hear?
59.8: But thou, O LORD, shalt have them in derision, * and thou shalt laugh all the heathen to scorn.
59.9: My strength will I ascribe unto thee; * for thou art the God of my refuge.
59.10: God showeth me his goodness plenteously; * and God shall let me see my desire upon mine enemies.
59.11: Slay them not, lest my people forget it; * but scatter them abroad among the people, and put them down, O Lord our defence.
59.12: For the sin of their mouth, and for the words of their lips, they shall be taken in their pride: * and why? their talk is of cursing and lies.
59.13: Consume them in thy wrath, consume them, that they may perish; * and know that it is God that ruleth in Jacob, and unto the ends of the world.
59.14: And in the evening they will return, * grin like a dog, and will go about the city.
59.15: They will run here and there for meat, * and grudge if they be not satisfied.
59.16: As for me, I will sing of thy power, and will praise thy mercy betimes in the morning; * for thou hast been my defence and refuge in the day of my trouble.
59.17: Unto thee, O my strength, will I sing; * for thou, O God, art my refuge, and my merciful God.

60.1: O God, thou hast cast us out, and scattered us abroad; * thou hast also been displeased: O turn thee unto us again.
60.2: Thou hast moved the land, and divided it: * heal the sores thereof, for it shaketh.
60.3: Thou hast showed thy people heavy things; * thou hast given us a drink of deadly wine.
60.4: Thou hast given a token for such as fear thee, * that they may triumph because of the truth.
60.5: Therefore were thy beloved delivered; * help me with thy right hand, and hear me.
60.6: God hath spoken in his holiness, I will rejoice, and divide Shechem, * and mete out the valley of Succoth.
60.7: Gilead is mine, and Manasseh is mine; * Ephraim also is the strength of my head; Judah is my law-giver;
60.8: Moab is my wash-pot; over Edom will I cast out my shoe; * Philistia, be thou glad of me.
60.9: Who will lead me into the strong city? * who will bring me into Edom?
60.10: Hast not thou cast us out, O God? * wilt not thou, O God, go out with our hosts?
60.11: O be thou our help in trouble; * for vain is the help of man.
60.12: Through God will we do great acts; * for it is he that shall tread down our enemies.

61.1: Hear my crying, O God, * give ear unto my prayer.
61.2: From the ends of the earth will I call upon thee, * when my heart is in heaviness.
61.3: O set me up upon the rock that is higher than I; * for thou hast been my hope, and a strong tower for me against the enemy.
61.4: I will dwell in thy tabernacle for ever, * and my trust shall be under the covering of thy wings.
61.5: For thou, O Lord, hast heard my desires, * and hast given an heritage unto those that fear thy Name.
61.6: Thou shalt grant the King a long life, * that his years may endure throughout all generations.
61.7: He shall dwell before God for ever: * O prepare thy loving mercy and faithfulness, that they may preserve him.
61.8: So will I alway sing praise unto thy Name, * that I may daily perform my vows.

62.1: My soul truly waiteth still upon God; * for of him | cometh | my sal- | vation.
62.2: He verily is my strength and my salvation; * he is my defence, so that I | shall not | greatly | fall.
62.3: How long will ye imagine mischief against every man? * Ye shall be slain all the sort of you; yea, as a tottering wall shall ye be, and | like a | broken | hedge.
62.4: Their device is only how to put him out whom God will exalt; * their delight is in lies; they give good words with their | mouth, but | curse with | their heart.
62.5: Nevertheless, my soul, wait thou still upon God; * for my | hope is | in | him.
62.6: He truly is my strength and my salvation; * he is my defence, so that | I shall | not | fall.
62.7: In God is my health and my glory; * the rock of my might; | and in | God is | my trust.
62.8: O put your trust in him alway, ye people; * pour out your hearts before him, for | God is | our | hope.
62.9: As for the children of men, they are but vanity; the children of men are deceitful; * upon the weights they are altogether lighter than | vani- | ty it- | self.
62.10: O trust not in wrong and robbery; give not yourselves unto vanity: * if riches increase, set | not your | heart up- | on them.
62.11: God spake once, and twice I have also heard the same, * that power be- | longeth | unto | God;
62.12: And that thou, Lord, art merciful; * for thou rewardest every man ac- | cording | to his | work.

63.1: O God, thou art my God; * early will I seek thee.
63.2: My soul thirsteth for thee, my flesh also longeth after thee, * in a barren and dry land where no water is.
63.3: Thus have I looked for thee in the sanctuary, * that I might behold thy power and glory.
63.4: For thy loving-kindness is better than life itself: * my lips shall praise thee.
63.5: As long as I live will I magnify thee in this manner, * and lift up my hands in thy Name.
63.6: My soul shall be satisfied, even as it were with marrow and fatness, * when my mouth praiseth thee with joyful lips.
63.7: Have I not remembered thee in my bed, * and thought upon thee when I was waking?
63.8: Because thou hast been my helper; * therefore under the shadow of thy wings will I rejoice.
63.9: My soul hangeth upon thee; * thy right hand hath upholden me.
63.10: These also that seek the hurt of my soul, * they shall go under the earth.
63.11: Let them fall upon the edge of the sword, * that they may be a portion for foxes.
63.12: But the King shall rejoice in God; all they also that swear by him shall be commended; * for the mouth of them that speak lies shall be stopped.

64.1: Hear my voice, O God, in my prayer; * preserve my life from fear of the enemy.
64.2: Hide me from the gathering together of the froward, * and from the insurrection of wicked doers;
64.3: Who have whet their tongue like a sword, * and shoot out their arrows, even bitter words;
64.4: That they may privily shoot at him that is perfect: * suddenly do they hit him, and fear not.
64.5: They encourage themselves in mischief, * and commune among themselves, how they may lay snares; and say, that no man shall see them.
64.6: They imagine wickedness, and practice it; * that they keep secret among themselves, every man in the deep of his heart.
64.7: But God shall suddenly shoot at them with a swift arrow, * that they shall be wounded.
64.8: Yea, their own tongues shall make them fall; * insomuch that whoso seeth them shall laugh them to scorn.
64.9: And all men that see it shall say, This hath God done; * for they shall perceive that it is his work.
64.10: The righteous shall rejoice in the LORD, and put his trust in him; * and all they that are true of heart shall be glad.

65.1: Thou, O God, art praised in Sion; * and unto thee shall the vow be performed in Jerusalem.
65.2: Thou that hearest the prayer, * unto thee shall all flesh come.
65.3: My misdeeds prevail against me: * O be thou merciful unto our sins.
65.4: Blèssed is the man whom thou choosest, and receivest unto thee: * he shall dwell in thy court, and shall be satisfied with the pleasures of thy house, even of thy holy temple.
65.5: Thou shalt show us wonderful things in thy righteousness, O God of our salvation; * thou that art the hope of all the ends of the earth, and of them that remain in the broad sea.
65.6: Who in his strength setteth fast the mountains, * and is girded about with power.
65.7: Who stilleth the raging of the sea, * and the noise of his waves, and the madness of the peoples.
65.8: They also that dwell in the uttermost parts of the earth shall be afraid at thy tokens, * thou that makest the out-goings of the morning and evening to praise thee.
65.9: Thou visitest the earth, and blessest it; * thou makest it very plenteous.
65.10: The river of God is full of water: * thou preparest their corn, for so thou providest for the earth.
65.11: Thou waterest her furrows; thou sendest rain into the little valleys thereof; * thou makest it soft with the drops of rain, and blessest the increase of it.
65.12: Thou crownest the year with thy goodness; * and thy clouds drop fatness.
65.13: They shall drop upon the dwellings of the wilderness; * and the little hills shall rejoice on every side.
65.14: The folds shall be full of sheep; * the valleys also shall stand so thick with corn, that they shall laugh and sing.
